title: Flash hazard near miss - GT2 electrical panel
description: Maintenance technician Marcus Whitley was performing I&C work on GT2 auxiliary panel. Panel was believed de-energized per lockout/tagout procedure but an adjacent 120V control circuit was not identified on the LOTO permit. No arc flash occurred - identified during pre-work voltage verification. LOTO permit updated. Full LOTO audit of all electrical equipment initiated.

title: Strain injury - GT2 HGP combustion hardware removal
description: GE field technician (contractor) reported lower back strain during combustion can removal. 30-lb can was being lowered manually without mechanical assist. Worker transported to occupational health. Returned to modified duty day 3. Full duty day 8. OSHA recordable. Revised procedure now requires mechanical assist for all combustion can handling.

title: Chemical splash - cooling water treatment
description: Plant operator Carlos Vega received minor splash to right forearm from sulfuric acid during chemical addition to CT makeup water system. PPE - chemical splash goggles and gloves were worn. No skin contact. First aid precautionary rinse. Reviewed chemical handling SOP and confirmed proper PPE protocol. No recordable.

title: Heat stress monitoring event
description: Two GE contractors reported heat stress symptoms during GT1 combustion inspection (ambient 102F). Escorted to cool area for recovery. No medical treatment required. Revised summer heat plan implemented - mandatory hydration stations 15-minute cool-down breaks every 90 minutes during work in confined turbine spaces.
